title: Innovative Malaysian Tofu Satay
description: A romantic Malaysian dish featuring coriander and fermented tofu, this innovative satay is egg-free-friendly and perfect for a night in, the combination of fermented and aerated elements creates a unique twist on traditional satay.
instructions: Cut the fermented tofu into small cubes and set aside.
Grind the coriander seeds into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat.
Add the garlic and ginger to the pan and saute until fragrant.
Add the coconut milk to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the tofu cubes to the pan and cook until golden brown.
Remove the tofu from the pan and thread onto skewers.
Brush the skewers with lemon juice and sprinkle with salt and coriander powder.
Serve the satay hot with peanut sauce for dipping.
Garnish with additional coriander seeds and lemon wedges if desired.
